note:
如果您不熟悉微控制器，则可以使用这些设备控制其他部件或其他设备。您可能会认为，使用常规的Raspberry Pi已经可以进行此类操作。但是，微控制器是专门为与其他事物交互而设计的。
 它们很便宜，体积很小，消耗的功率也很小。您可以使用面包板开始开发项目，以避免焊接。您可以将它与小电池配对使用，并且可以运行数周甚至数月。与计算机不同，微控制器不运行传统的操作系统。您的代码直接在芯片上运行。
  像其他微控制器一样，Raspberry Pi Pico在设备的侧面具有数十个输入和输出引脚。这些引脚很重要，因为它们充当与其他组件的接口。例如，您可以使您的微控制器与LED灯互动，从各种传感器获取数据，在显示器上显示一些信息等。
 Raspberry Pi Pico使用RP2040芯片。它具有一个双核Arm处理器（以133MHz运行），264KB的RAM，26个GPIO引脚（包括3个模拟输入），一个微型USB端口和一个温度传感器。它没有Wi-Fi或蓝牙。它的价格是4美元。
 如果您想在Raspberry Pi Pico上运行某些程序，这很容易。您使用微型USB端口将设备插入计算机。您可以在按下按钮的同时启动Raspberry Pi Pico。该设备将作为外部驱动器出现在您的计算机上。
 除了C，您还可以使用MicroPython作为开发语言。这是微控制器的Python启发式语言。 Raspberry Pi基金会已经为Pico编写了大量文档和数据表。
 有趣的是，Raspberry Pi基金会希望让其他人从其自己的芯片设计中受益。它已经接触到Adafruit，Arduino，Pimoroni和Sparkfun，以便他们可以使用RP2040芯片构建自己的板。将会有一个由RP2040驱动的设备的整个生态系统。 
对于Raspberry Pi Foundation而言，这是一个有趣的举动，因为它可以沿着这条道路前进，并以更强大的变体迭代自己的芯片设计。 它提供了两个主要优势-精确控制要放置的东西的能力和价格。
note_en:
If you’re not familiar with microcontrollers, those devices let you control other parts or other devices. You might think that you can already do this kind of stuff with  a regular Raspberry Pi. But microcontrollers are specifically designed to interact with other things.
 They’re cheap, they’re small and they draw very little power. You can start developing your project with a breadboard to avoid soldering. You can pair it with a small battery and it can run for weeks or even months. Unlike computers, microcontrollers don’t run traditional operating systems. Your code runs directly on the chip.
  Like other microcontrollers, the Raspberry Pi Pico has dozens of input and output pins on the sides of the device. Those pins are important as they act as the interface with other components. For instance, you can make your microcontroller interact with an LED light, get data from various sensors, show some information on a display, etc.
 The Raspberry Pi Pico uses the RP2040 chip. It has a dual-core Arm processor (running at 133MHz), 264KB of RAM, 26 GPIO pins including 3 analog inputs, a micro-USB port and a temperature sensor. It doesn’t come with Wi-Fi or Bluetooth. And it costs $4.
 If you want to run something on the Raspberry Pi Pico, it’s quite easy. You plug your device to your computer using the micro-USB port. You boot up the Raspberry Pi Pico while pressing the button. The device will appear on your computer as an external drive.
 In addition to C, you can use MicroPython as your development language. It’s a Python-inspired language for microcontrollers. The Raspberry Pi Foundation has written a ton of  documentation and a  datasheet for the Pico.
 Interestingly, the Raspberry Pi Foundation wants to let others benefit from its own chip design. It has reached out to Adafruit, Arduino, Pimoroni and Sparkfun so that they can build their own boards using the RP2040 chip. There will be an entire ecosystem of RP2040-powered devices.
 This is an interesting move for the Raspberry Pi Foundation as it can go down this path and iterate on its own chip design with more powerful variants. It provides two main advantages — the ability to control exactly what to put on board, and price.
title: Raspberry Pi Foundation推出具有定制芯片的4美元微控制器
title_en: Raspberry Pi Foundation launches $4 microcontroller with custom chip

新生儿的皮肤是蓝色的，他没有呼吸。几年前，医生会把婴儿记录为死胎，不相信他们可以做些什么来帮助。如果这是在1950年代中期，那么妇产科学领域的最新发展将会给他们带来希望-Apgar评分。新生儿的1分钟Apgar得分表明该新生儿状况不佳，但是他们给了他氧气。果然，他的5分钟Apgar得分有所改善。也许他毕竟有机会。
  弗吉尼亚·阿普加（Virginia Apgar）于1909年6月7日出生于新泽西州的韦斯特菲尔德。尽管她父亲的日常工作是保险公司的高管，但从侧面看，他是一名业余发明家和天文学家。小时候，弗吉尼亚·阿普加（Virginia Apgar）学会了拉小提琴，后来加入了她的高中乐队。大约在这个时候，她把目光投向了医学。她在霍利奥克山学院（Mount Holyoke College）攻读动物学学位，并被誉为杰出学生。
 1929年毕业后，Apgar在哥伦比亚大学内科医生与外科医生学院开始了医学培训。当时，该领域的女性人数严重不足；她的90名同学中只有八名其他女性。 1933年获得医学博士学位后，她成为哥伦比亚大学内科医生与外科医生学院的首批女性外科手术住院医师之一。但是，她的导师，外科医生艾伦·惠普勒（Allen Whipple）担心，由于她是女性，因此很难吸引患者。由于这些反馈，Apgar转向麻醉学，这是1940年代中期建立的相对较新的专业。这不是一个享有盛誉的职业道路，但是由于麻醉术主要是由当时的护士来处理的，因此性别歧视不会带来太大的障碍。
  作为麻醉师，阿普加尔甚至没有分娩过婴儿，因此不太可能改变产科领域。但是，她的一部分工作是为分娩提供麻醉，因此她有大量接触新生儿的机会。在1950年代，降低婴儿死亡率（婴儿在其第一个生日之前的死亡）是一个艰巨的问题：每30个新生儿中就有一个惊人的婴儿在出生时死亡。
 那时，如果新生婴儿似乎并没有蓬勃发展-例如，如果婴儿的皮肤太蓝或被认为太小-婴儿将被杀死并被记录为死胎。当然，并不是所有人都希望这些婴儿死亡，但医生只是认为他们病得太重，无法生存。 Apgar相信，如果这样的婴儿得到更好的照顾，他们中的许多人都会活下来。
 由于Apgar是一位“低下”的麻醉师，她无法直接挑战产科医生的工作方式。而且，她是男人世界中的女人。尽管如此，她还是设计了“ Apgar分数”。 –观察和记录每个新生儿状况的方法。这是一种简单的间接方法，在帮助显着降低婴儿死亡率方面产生了有力的结果。
  Apgar得分是首字母缩写词，每个字母代表得分的组成部分：活动，脉搏，鬼脸，外观和呼吸。换句话说，该分数评估了新生儿的运动，心律，烦躁，肤色和呼吸。在每个类别中，新生儿获得0、1或2分。例如，在“脉搏”类别中，新生儿的脉搏可能不存在（0分），每分钟100次以下（1分）或每分钟100次以上（2分）。总分在0到10之间，低分表示新生儿状况不佳，高分表示新生儿状况良好。 
Apgar评分使医疗保健提供者可以系统地观察和记录每个新生儿的状况-从婴儿出生后一分钟开始，到婴儿出生后五分钟再开始。实施Apgar评分引入了竞争精神，因为医生天生就希望他们分娩的新生儿获得更好的评分。显而易见，低分的婴儿起初可以显着改善，并且在出生后五分钟内具有高分。
 此外，Apgar与儿科医生L. Stanley James和麻醉师Duncan Holaday等同事合作，为Apgar评分对降低婴儿死亡率的深远影响建立了生理基础。通过分析新生儿的血液化学，例如新生儿血液中的氧气和二氧化碳水平，Apgar和其他人能够更好地将新生儿的Apgar得分与分娩，分娩和产妇麻醉实践的效果相关联。在美国今天，很大程度上要归功于Apgar在妇产科学领域的贡献，每500名新生儿中只有大约三分之二没有活着看到他们的第一个生日。
 如今，Apgar评分仍然是护理的标准，尽管有些人想知道它是否在现代医学中仍然有意义。然而，尽管有人建议过高的Apgar评分标准，但2013年的这项研究得出的结论是，Apgar评分“对于预测新生儿和新生儿后的不良结局具有持续的价值。”
  在为超过17,000名婴儿进行分娩的过程中，Apgar看到了许多有出生缺陷的新生儿，她随后成为新兴的畸形学领域–出生缺陷研究的领导者。 1958年，她从临床实践中休假，并在约翰·霍普金斯大学（Johns Hopkins University）取得公共卫生硕士学位。随后，她成为了现在的Dimes游行的部门主管。在担任该职务期间，Apgar致力于增加畸形学领域的研究，以最大程度地帮助预防和改善出生缺陷。她的医学训练加上难以置信的决心和毅力使她在医学领域留下了令人鼓舞的遗产。
 Apgar从未退休，但她患上了进行性肝病并于1974年8月7日去世。除了她一生中获得无数奖项外，她的贡献也得到了广泛的追悼。 1994年，她的肖像被列入美国纪念邮票系列“伟大的美国人”。次年，阿普加入选了国家妇女名人堂。
note_en:
The newborn’s skin was blue and he wasn’t breathing. A few years earlier, the doctors would have documented the baby as stillborn, not believing there was anything they could do to help. If this were the mid-1950s though, a recent development in the field of obstetrics would have given them hope – the Apgar score. The newborn’s 1-minute Apgar score indicated that the newborn was in poor condition, but they treated him with oxygen. Sure enough, his 5-minute Apgar score showed improvement. Maybe he had a chance after all.
  Virginia Apgar was born on June 7, 1909 in Westfield, New Jersey. Although her father’s day job was that of an insurance executive, on the side he was an amateur inventor and astronomer. As a child, Virginia Apgar learned to play the violin and later joined her high school orchestra. Around this time, she set her sights on medicine. At Mount Holyoke College, she pursued a degree in zoology and was lauded as an  exceptional student.
 After graduating in 1929, Apgar began her medical training at Columbia University's College of Physicians and Surgeons. Women were drastically underrepresented in the field at the time; there were  only eight other women in her class of 90 people. After completing her MD in 1933, she became one of the first female surgical residents at Columbia University College of Physicians and Surgeons. However, her mentor, surgeon Allen Whipple, was concerned that since she was a woman, she would have a hard time attracting patients. As a result of this feedback,  Apgar pivoted to anesthesiology, a relatively new specialty established in the mid-1940s. This was a much less prestigious career path, but since anesthesia was mostly handled by nurses at the time, gender discrimination wouldn't present as much of a hurdle.
  As an anesthesiologist, Apgar had never even delivered a baby and was therefore an unlikely candidate for revolutionizing the field of obstetrics. However, part of her job entailed providing anesthesia for deliveries, so she had plenty of exposure to newborns. In the 1950s, reducing infant mortality (the death of an infant before their first birthday) was a daunting problem: an astonishing  one in 30 newborns died at birth.
 At that time, if a newborn didn’t seem to be thriving – if, for example, the baby’s skin was too blue or they were deemed too small –  the baby would be left to die and documented as a stillborn. Of course, it wasn’t that anyone wanted these babies to die, but doctors simply believed that they were too sick to survive. Apgar believed that if such babies received better care, many of them would live.
 Since Apgar was a “lowly” anesthesiologist, she wasn’t in a position to directly challenge how obstetricians did their work. Moreover, she was a woman in a man’s world. Nevertheless, she devised the "Apgar score" – a way to observe and document the condition of every newborn. It was a simple, indirect approach that had powerful results in terms of helping to dramatically decrease the infant mortality rate.
  The Apgar score is an acronym with each letter standing for a component of the score: Activity, Pulse, Grimace, Appearance, and Respiration. In other words, the score evaluates a newborn’s movement, heart rate, irritability, color, and breathing. In each category, a newborn receives 0, 1, or 2 points. In the “Pulse” category, for example, the newborn’s pulse may either be absent (0 points), below 100 beats per minutes (1 point), or over 100 beats per minute (2 points). The total score ranges from 0-10, with a low score indicating a newborn in poor condition, and a high score indicating a newborn in excellent condition.
 The Apgar score enables healthcare providers to systematically observe and document the condition of every newborn – starting one minute after the baby is born and again five minutes after birth. Implementing the Apgar score introduced  a spirit of competition because the doctors inherently wanted the newborns they delivered to have better scores. It became readily apparent that a baby with a low score initially could improve remarkably and have an excellent score five minutes after birth.
 Moreover, Apgar worked with colleagues such as pediatrician L. Stanley James and anesthesiologist Duncan Holaday to establish the physiological basis for the Apgar score’s profound effect on reducing infant mortality. By analyzing neonatal blood chemistry, such as the oxygen and carbon dioxide levels in the newborn's blood, Apgar and others were better able  to correlate a newborn’s Apgar scores to the effects of labor, delivery, and maternal anesthesia practices. Today in the US, thanks in large part to Apgar’s contribution to the field of obstetrics, only about  three out of five hundred newborns don’t live to see their first birthday.
 Today the Apgar score remains the standard of care, although some have wondered whether or not it is still pertinent in the context of modern medicine. However, despite suggestions that the Apgar score may be antiquated,  this study from 2013 concluded that the Apgar score “has continuing value for predicting neonatal and post-neonatal adverse outcomes.”
  In the process of attending over 17,000 births, Apgar had seen many newborns with birth defects, and she went on to become a leader in the emerging field of teratology – the study of birth defects. In 1958, she took a sabbatical from clinical practice and pursued a master's degree in public health from Johns Hopkins University. Subsequently, she became the director of a division at what is now the March of Dimes. In this role, Apgar worked to increase research in the field of teratology in order to help prevent and ameliorate birth defects to the greatest extent possible. Her medical training coupled with incredible determination and perseverance enabled her to leave  an inspiring legacy in the field of medicine.
 Apgar never retired, but she developed progressive liver disease and died on August 7, 1974. In addition to receiving numerous awards during her lifetime, her contributions have also been recognized far and wide posthumously. In 1994, her portrait was included in the  commemorative U.S. postage stamp series of “Great Americans.” The following year, Apgar was inducted into  the National Women's Hall of Fame.
title: Apgar评分有助于降低婴儿死亡率
title_en: The Apgar Score helped decrease the infant mortality rate

 拜登总统就职后，白宫网站获得了重大更新。该网站包含有关现任政府和白宫历史的信息，但隐藏在其代码中是暗中邀请人们​​申请技术工作的信息。
 如果您使用浏览器上的开发人员工具来检查网站，则会看到一条隐藏的消息，指出，如果您正在阅读本文，我们需要您的帮助以更好地重建。＆＃34 ;然后，它具有美国数字服务（USDS）职位申请页面的文本。
 USDS是一个技术部门，在整个美国联邦政府中致力于改善服务。人们可以加入USDS进行为期四年的公民服务之旅。根据美国农业部的数据，大多数涉案人员服务一年或两年。
  USDS部署了由设计师，工程师，产品经理和官僚机构组成的小组，以帮助公务员。
   USDS页面介绍了招聘过程和与组织相关人员的要求。由于当前的全球大流行，目前USDS的大多数人都在远程工作，但通常人们需要靠近华盛顿特区。
 新的白宫网站还具有深色模式，高对比度爱好者可能会喜欢它。 
一种新的Surface Laptop设备被发现通过了蓝牙认证。 该设备似乎是Surface Laptop 3+或Surface Laptop 4。  我曾经爱过Surface，但经过多年的麻烦，我却不知所措。 这就是为什么。  开发人员最近设法使Windows 10X在多个不受支持的设备上运行。 现在，有一个视频展示了Surface Pro 7上的Windows 10X。  您不需要台式机即可真正享受Microsoft Flight Simulator2020。我们已经搜集了一些出色的游戏笔记本电脑，例如出色的Razer Blade 15，它将使您不需花太多时间就可以开心地上山。 台。
note_en:
The new White House website includes a hidden message inviting people to apply to the U.S. Digital Service.
 Following the inauguration of President Biden, the  White House website received a major update. The website includes information about the current administration and the history of the White House but hidden in its code is a secret invitation for people to apply for a job in technology.
 If you use the developer tools on your browser to inspect the website, you'll see a hidden message that states, "If you're reading this, we need your help building back better." It then has the text for the  United States Digital Service (USDS) job application page.
 The USDS is a technology unit that works across the U.S. federal government to improve services. People can join the USDS in tours of civic service with a maximum term of four years. The majority of people involved serve for one or two years, according to the USDS.
  The USDS deploys groups of designers, engineers, product managers, and bureaucracy specialists to help civil servants.
   The USDS page goes over the hiring process and requirements for people to be involved with the organization. The majority of people at the USDS are working remotely at the moment due to the current global pandemic but normally people need to be near Washing, D.C.
 The new White House website also has a dark mode, which will likely be appreciated by lovers of high contrast.
  A new Surface Laptop device was spotted on a Bluetooth certification. The device appears to be either the Surface Laptop 3+ or the Surface Laptop 4.
 I used to love Surface, but after years of problems, I've thrown in the towel. Here's why.
 Developers recently managed to get Windows 10X running on several unsupported devices. Now, there's a video showing Windows 10X on a Surface Pro 7.
 You don't require a desktop PC to truly enjoy Microsoft Flight Simulator 2020. We've rounded up some fine gaming laptops, like the excellent Razer Blade 15, that will more than happily let you take to the skies without a desk.
title: 新的白宫网站上有一条隐藏的消息，要求编码员
title_en: The new White House website has a hidden message calling for coders

最初在努力筹集资金以打开大门后，阿里斯市的ONA餐厅获得了奖励
    法国西南部的一家素食餐厅赢得了米其林星，这是法国第一家仅提供无动物产品的餐厅。
 克莱尔·瓦莱（ClaireVallée）在波尔多附近的阿瑞斯（Ares）市经营ONA餐厅-该餐厅代表Origine Non Animale-她于2016年成立，得益于支持者的众筹和绿色银行的贷款。
  瓦莱（Vallée）告诉法新社，当她接到米其林指南的电话通知她作出决定的那一刻时，“感觉就像被火车撞了一样。”
 除了周一获得的经典之星奖之外，瓦莱还获得了一颗绿色之星。米其林去年推出了这一绿色之星，以奖励在道德规范方面表现出色的企业。
 米其林指南女发言人告诉法新社，ONA是“法国第一家获得星级的纯素食餐厅”。
  由于Covid-19的限制，在不得不关闭ONA之前，Vallée在美食菜单上提供了7种菜肴。她最喜欢的组合包括松木，牛肝菌蘑菇和清酒，或芹菜，零陵香豆和琥珀啤酒。 
传统的法国银行在给年轻厨师寻求贷款以开始贷款时让她轻描淡写。 她说：“他们说素食主义和植物性食品的前景太不确定了。” 她在大西洋海岸阿卡雄盆地的餐厅所选择的地点也被认为前景不佳。  瓦莱（Vallée）继续通过众筹（无需抵押）以及通过专门研究其认为合乎道德的项目的银行La Nef来确保融资。 她说：“这表明没有什么是不可能的。”  瓦莱（Vallée）承认一直在思考“我们是否足够好，因为以蔬菜为基础的烹饪既困难又创新”。  尽管现在被认为是法国素食烹饪的先驱，但瓦莱（Vallée）表示，她只是跟随别人的脚步，挑选出已故柏林经营Mano Verde餐厅的让-克里斯蒂安（Jean-Christian Jury）陪审团。
note_en:
Restaurant ONA in the city of Ares rewarded after initially struggling to get funding to open its doors
    A vegan restaurant in south-west  France has won a Michelin star, the first for an establishment serving only animal-free products in  France.
 Claire Vallée runs the restaurant ONA – which stands for  Origine Non Animale – in the city of Ares, near Bordeaux, which she launched in 2016 thanks to crowdfunding from supporters and a loan from a green bank.
  “It felt like I got hit by a train,” Vallée told AFP about the moment she received a call from the Guide Michelin informing her of its decision.
 In addition to Monday’s award of the classic star, Vallée also won a green star, which Michelin introduced last year to reward establishments with a strong record for ethical practices.
 ONA is “the first vegan restaurant in France to win a star”, a Guide Michelin spokeswoman told AFP.
  Vallée offered seven dishes on her gourmet menu before she had to close ONA because of Covid-19 restrictions. Her favourite combinations involve pine, boletus mushroom and sake, or celery, tonka and amber ale.
 Traditional French banks gave the young chef short shrift when she came to them looking for a loan to get started. “They said the outlook for veganism and plant-based food was too uncertain,” she said. The chosen location for her restaurant in the Arcachon basin on the Atlantic coast was also not considered promising enough.
  Vallée went on to secure financing through crowdfunding, without the need for collateral, and through La Nef, a bank that specialises in lending to projects it considers ethical. “This goes to show that nothing is impossible,” she said.
 Vallée admitted to having wondered along the way “whether we were good enough because vegetable-based cooking is difficult and innovative”.
  Although now considered a pioneer for vegan cuisine in France, Vallée said she simply followed in the footsteps of others, singling out the late Jean-Christian Jury, who ran the Mano Verde restaurant in Berlin.
title: 法国第一位素食米其林星
title_en: First vegan michelin star in France

该研究测试了公众支持在缺粮500年后重新带回物种，而农民则担心绵羊
    一群希望将野生山猫释放到苏格兰高地的环保主义者发起了一项为期一年的研究，以了解公众是否支持其重新引入。
 这项研究由高地的两名亿万富翁丹麦房地产所有者，安德斯·波夫森（Anders Povlsen）和利斯贝特·劳辛（Lisbet Rausing）部分资助，将测试农民，土地所有者和农村社区是否同意在苏格兰偏远地区进行试点项目。
  欧洲最大的土猫是山猫，在500年前由于栖息地丧失，狩猎和迫害而在北英国灭绝，但其他野心勃勃的倡导者提出的重新引入英国的提议遭到破坏。
 环保人士认为，由文森特野生动物基金会（Vincent Wildlife Trust）和生命之树（Trees for Life）与苏格兰：大图片组织（Scotland：The Big Picture）的活动家共同开展的新项目很有可能获得成功。
 苏格兰：大局局长彼得·凯恩斯（Pete Cairns）说：“我们的角色是推动流程前进，以有意义的方式接力棒。已经进行了一些研究来评估带回山猫的生态可行性。从很多方面来说，这都是容易的事。
 “捕食者众多的问题不在于科学，而在于人们的态度，信念和优先事项。任何关于山猫的讨论的先决条件是，我们需要知道苏格兰公众对此表示支持，并将容忍这一点。” 
该计划面临来自苏格兰农民的强烈反对，苏格兰农民援引挪威约2万只绵羊向其本土掠食者金刚狼，天猫，熊和狼的损失。苏格兰全国农民联合会（NFUS）表示，山猫被追究了五分之一的损失。
 NFUS副主席马丁·肯尼迪（Martin Kennedy）说：“在过去的十年中，挪威的捕食活动有所减少-不是因为捕食者减少了，而是因为山地农民已经停止养羊了。
 “挪威人告诉我们，将掠食者重新引入我国将是一场绝对的灾难。他们的经验仅仅增强了我们的决心。”
 人们认为，如果名为“苏格兰天猫座”的调查和参与活动成功地提供了足够的公众支持，则可以在大约五年内申请将最多40只山猫释放到野外的许可证。
 在凯恩戈姆斯，一簇茂密的高地庄园，包括波夫森和阿伯内西的皇家鸟类保护组织拥有的庄园，被视为最有可能进行试点的地点。
 现在是苏格兰最大的私人土地所有者的Rausing和Povlsen倡导在他们的庄园进行野蛮项目，着手进行造林项目，禁止驱赶松鸡射击，鹿控制计划以及保护受迫害的猛禽。
 ly将被贴上标签并受到密切监视，这反映了一项长期运行的官方计划，以重新引入苏格兰阿盖尔州纳普代尔森林的海狸家庭，该计划始于2009年，尽管遭到了一些当地地主的反对。 
凯恩斯说，合伙企业目前不考虑许可申请。 “在12或14个月后，可能有证据表明苏格兰公众尚未准备好接受山猫。在这种情况下，我们必须走开它。”凯恩斯说。
 人们担心，除非官方计划集结动力，否则野心勃勃的激进主义者可能试图在苏格兰非法释放山猫。
 Knapdale海狸项目很快被在Tayside的农民的苦难行所笼罩，这是由于非法或意外释放的海狸在该地区迅速传播所致。在2019年的许可扑杀的前八个月中，有近90只海狸在许可下被枪杀，其他许多则被秘密杀害。
 再次引入山猫计划面临的最重大障碍是，要满足政府保护机构NatureScot和国际自然保护联盟规定的严格的环境和政策测试。
 该机构的规定要求执照申请者证明新物种可以自然生存，但其释放也通过对“人们的福祉，生计和娱乐活动造成不可接受的损害”，避免与绵羊或养鸡场等当地土地使用者发生冲突。
 爱丁堡大学的地理学家弗雷泽·麦克唐纳（Fraser MacDonald）博士专门研究土地利用和人口，他说，山猫项目必须消除人们的担忧，即它在强大的土地所有者的利益的推动下提倡一种野蛮的精英主义观念。
 麦克唐纳德说：“如果要使生态恢复取得成功（要想在2021年实现远胜于胆大妄想的生存），那么就必须从那些可能受到影响的社区中获得支持。” “其影响深远，以至于需要更多的民主审查。”
note_en:
Study tests public support for bringing back species after 500-year absence, while farmers fear for sheep
    A consortium of conservationists that hopes to release wild lynx into the Scottish Highlands has launched a year-long study to see whether the public supports their reintroduction.
 The study, part-funded by two billionaire Danish estate owners in the Highlands, Anders Povlsen and Lisbet Rausing, will test whether farmers, landowners and rural communities will agree to a pilot project in a remote area of  Scotland.
  The lynx, Europe’s largest native cat, became extinct in northern Britain more than 500 years ago through habitat loss, hunting and persecution, but proposals by other rewilding advocates to reintroduce the species into the UK have foundered.
 Conservationists believe the new project, run by the Vincent Wildlife Trust and Trees for Life, alongside campaigners at Scotland: The Big Picture, has a significant chance of success.
 Pete Cairns, the director of Scotland: The Big Picture, said: “Our role is to get the process moving, to pick up the baton in a meaningful way. There have been several studies to assess the ecological feasibility of bringing back lynx; that’s the easy bit in many ways.
 “The question with a large predator is less about science and more about people’s attitudes, beliefs and priorities. A prerequisite for any discussion over lynx is we need to know the Scottish public are in support of this and would tolerate this.”
 The scheme faces heavy opposition from Scottish farmers, who cite the loss of about 20,000 sheep in Norway to its native predators, the wolverine, lynx, bear and wolf. The National Farmers Union of Scotland (NFUS) said lynx were blamed for a fifth of those losses.
 Martin Kennedy, the deputy vice-president of the NFUS, said: “Predation in Norway has reduced over the past decade – not because of fewer predators, but the fact that hill farmers have simply stopped keeping sheep.
 “The Norwegians told us that to reintroduce predators into our country would be an absolute catastrophe. Their experience has simply strengthened our resolve.”
 It is thought that if the survey and engagement campaign, called Lynx for Scotland, succeeds in providing enough public support, an application for a licence to release the first of up to 40 lynx into the wild could be made in about five years.
 A cluster of densely forested Highland estates in the Cairngorms, including estates owned by Povlsen and the Royal Society for the Protection of Birds reserve at Abernethy, are seen as the most likely sites for a pilot project.
 Rausing and Povlsen, now Scotland’s largest private landowners, have championed rewilding projects on their estates, embarking on reforesting projects, banning driven grouse shooting, deer control programmes and conserving persecuted birds of prey.
 The lynx would be tagged and closely monitored, mirroring a long-running official programme to reintroduce families of beavers in Scotland at Knapdale forest in Argyll, which  began in 2009 despite vocal opposition by some local landowners.
 Cairns said the partnership was not considering a licence application at this stage. “After 12 or 14 months, it may be that the evidence suggests the Scottish public isn’t yet ready for lynx. In which case we have to walk away from it,” Cairns said.
 There are fears that rewilding activists could attempt to release lynx in Scotland unlawfully unless an official scheme gathers momentum.
 The Knapdale beaver project was quickly overshadowed by a bitter row with farmers in Tayside about the rapid spread of beavers released illegally or accidentally in the area. Almost 90 beavers  were shot under licence in the first eight months of licensed culling in 2019, with many others secretly killed.
 The most significant hurdle facing a lynx reintroduction programme is meeting strict environmental and policy tests laid down by NatureScot, the government conservation agency, and the International Union for the Conservation of Nature.
 The agency’s rules require licence applicants to prove the new species can survive naturally but its release also avoid conflicts with local land users, such as sheep or chicken farmers, by causing “unacceptable harm to people’s wellbeing, livelihoods and recreational activities”.
 Dr Fraser MacDonald, a geographer at the University of Edinburgh who specialises in land use and population, said the lynx project had to allay fears it was promoting an elitist concept of rewilding, driven by the interests of powerful landowners.
 “If ecological restoration is to succeed – to survive as more than a laird’s whim in 2021 – then it has to build support from precisely those communities who are likely to be affected,” Macdonald said. “Its implications are so far-reaching that it requires much greater democratic scrutiny.”

欧洲隐私监管机构正在审查雇主如何收集工人的个人数据，并对违法行为处以数百万美元的罚款。
  德国电子产品零售商notebooksbilliger.de是最新的目标公司。德国下萨克森州的数据保护监管机构称，在网上以及在实体商店中出售笔记本电脑，电话和其他电子产品的卖方因使用视频监控摄像头监视员工而被罚款1,040万欧元，相当于1,260万美元。这个月。
  该案反映出欧洲当局对雇主使用技术来监控员工的兴趣日益浓厚。 10月，另一家德国监管机构对时装零售商AB处以3,530万欧元（4,100万美元）的罚款，原因是该公司从员工那里收集个人数据，包括有关其健康和宗教信仰的详细信息，并至少在德国H＆amp; M服务中心向经理提供这些数据。 5年。监管机构表示，近年来，他们收到了更多关于违反工作场所隐私的投诉。隐私和就业专家说，随着美国和欧洲的雇主在大流行期间使用技术监视远程工人，对工人的监视似乎正在增加。
  下萨克森州隐私监管机构的芭芭拉·泰尔（Barbara Thiel）在电子邮件中表示，Notebooksbilliger.de违反了欧盟27个国家/地区的隐私法中的2018年通用数据保护条例，因为视频监视对于监视员工是不必要的。蒂尔女士说：“员工有权进行职业活动，而不受永久监督。”
    使用视频监视的公司必须证明为什么有必要避免违反GDPR，这对雇主而言可能是棘手的。欧盟数据保护监管机构的保护伞组织在去年发布的视频监控指南中表示，在大多数情况下，员工希望不受监控。
  这家电子产品零售商表示，将对其处以罚款，这是运输和物流公司使用摄像头跟踪商品存储和销售方式以及是否损坏或丢失的标准。该公司表示，它没有使用摄像头监控员工绩效。首席执行官奥利弗·赫尔莫尔德（Oliver Hellmold）说：“罚款完全不相称。”
  欧洲监管机构已表示，他们正在密切关注违反工人监督的行为。丹麦隐私权机构上个月发布了指南，概述了雇主应如何处理现有和以前的工人以及他们正在招聘的数据，法国监管机构于11月发布了对常见问题的答复，这些常见问题涉及雇主在员工远程工作时应如何处理隐私问题。 。法国监管者说，其中一条关键规则是：员工不能通过使用摄像机或按键记录器技术来持续监控他们的键入内容。 
上个月，阿尔巴尼亚数据保护监管机构建议经营客户和技术支持服务的法国SE公司停止使用网络摄像头监视在家工作的员工。 Teleperformance和阿尔巴尼亚监管机构未回应置评请求。
    布鲁金斯学会副总裁兼治理研究部主任达雷尔·韦斯特（Darrell M.West）说，在美国，只要员工使用公司的设备或在公司的网络上工作，就很少有法律规定可以阻止此类监视。他说，随着人们在远程工作，以及许多人可能会使用公司设备进行私人对话的工作和个人生活之间的界限越来越模糊，对员工进行监视的风险正在增加。
  West先生说，用于监视员工的电子邮件及其在社交媒体和在线协作工具中的活动的工具是常见且易于使用的。许多公司特别关注保持人们在冠状病毒大流行期间远程工作时的生产率，并使用技术跟踪其活动或完成任务需要多长时间。
    欧洲服务工人工会UNI Europa的欧洲区域秘书奥利弗·罗伊西格（Oliver Roethig）表示，雇主对工人的监督在服务行业尤其是在仓库中很普遍。
    Roethig先生说，欧洲的监管机构需要澄清公司如何在发展过程中使用某些技术，例如人工智能。他说，例如，可以将监视与算法结合起来，以尝试预测员工的行为，包括采取步骤加入工会，并告知有关如何对待这些人的决定。
  律师事务所霍根·洛弗斯（Hogan Lovells）伦敦办事处的合伙人斯蒂芬·马丁（Stefan Martin）表示，许多公司在开始使用新技术时会犯错误的步骤，因为他们不了解法律风险，而高级管理人员可能不知道该工具的实施。他补充说，经理们通常并不知道他们公司正在使用的特别危险的工具，例如用于监视公司计算机上员工行为的技术或可能歧视某些群体的面部识别工具。
  他说：“很多人都采用了技术来监视他们可能不理解的数据。”

European privacy regulators are scrutinizing how employers collect workers’ personal data and dishing out multimillion-dollar fines for violations.
  German electronics retailer notebooksbilliger.de is the latest company to be targeted. The seller of laptops, phones and other electronics online and in bricks-and-mortar shops was fined 10.4 million euros, equivalent to $12.6 million, for using video surveillance cameras to monitor employees, the data protection regulator in the German state of Lower Saxony said this month.
  The case reflects European authorities’ growing interest in employers’ use of technology to monitor employees. In October,  a different German regulator fined fashion retailer   AB €35.3 million ($41 million) for collecting personal data from employees, including details about their health and religion, and making them available to managers at a German H&M service center over at least five years. Regulators say they have received more complaints about workplace privacy violations in recent years. Worker surveillance appears to be increasing as employers in the U.S. and Europe use technologies to monitor remote workers during the pandemic, privacy and employment experts say.
  Notebooksbilliger.de violated the 2018 General Data Protection Regulation, the privacy law in the European Union’s 27 countries, because video surveillance was unnecessary to monitor employees, Barbara Thiel, the Lower Saxony privacy regulator said in an email. “Employees are entitled to perform their professional activities without being subject to permanent surveillance,” Ms. Thiel said.
    Companies using video surveillance must justify why it is necessary to avoid violating the GDPR, and that can be tricky for employers. In most cases, an employee expects not to be monitored, the umbrella group of EU data protection regulators said in guidelines on video surveillance published last year.
  The electronics retailer said it is appealing the fine and that it is standard for shipping and logistics companies to use cameras to track how goods are stored and sold, and whether they are damaged or go missing. The company said it didn’t use cameras to monitor employee performance. “The fine is completely disproportionate,” Chief Executive Oliver Hellmold said.
  European regulators have indicated they are paying closer attention to worker surveillance violations. The Danish privacy authority issued guidelines last month outlining how employers should handle data of current and former workers, as well as those they are recruiting, and the French regulator in November published responses to common questions about how employers should handle privacy issues while employees work remotely. One crucial rule: Employees can’t be under constant surveillance through the use of video cameras or key logger technologies that capture everything they type, the French regulator said.
  Last month, the Albanian data protection regulator recommended that French firm   SE, which operates customer and technical support services, stop using webcams to monitor employees working from home. Teleperformance and the Albanian regulator didn’t respond to requests for comment.
    In the U.S., there are few legal restrictions preventing such surveillance as long as employees are using a company device or working on a corporate network, said Darrell M. West, vice president and director of governance studies at the Brookings Institution. The risk of employee surveillance is increasing as people work remotely and the line between work and personal life blurs for many employees, who may use corporate devices for private conversations, he said.
  Tools to monitor employees’ email and their activity on social media and in online collaboration tools are common and easy to use, Mr. West said. Many companies are especially interested in keeping tabs on how productive people are while working remotely during the coronavirus pandemic, and use technologies to track their activity or how long it takes them to complete tasks.
    Employers’ surveillance of workers is common in service sectors and especially in warehouses, said Oliver Roethig, regional secretary for Europe at UNI Europa, the European service workers union.
    Regulators in Europe need to clarify how companies can use certain technologies as they evolve, such as artificial intelligence, Mr. Roethig said. For example, surveillance could be combined with algorithms to try to predict employee behavior, including taking steps to join a labor union, and inform decisions about how to treat those people, he said.
  Many companies make missteps when they start using new technology because they are unaware of legal risks, and senior executives may not know the tool was implemented, said Stefan Martin, a partner in the London office of law firm Hogan Lovells. Managers commonly aren’t aware of particularly risky tools that their company is using, such as technology to monitor employee behavior on corporate computers or a facial-recognition tool that could discriminate against certain groups, he added.
  “A lot of people put in place technology to monitor data that they potentially don’t understand,” he said.
title: 欧盟监管机构正在审查雇主如何通过视频监控或关键记录器技术来监控工人，以及此类行为是否违反GDPR
title_en: EU regulators are scrutinizing how employers monitor workers, including via video surveillance or key logger tech, and whether such actions violate GDPR

在1950年代后期，美国海军尝试了搭载喷气战斗机的潜艇飞机概念。作为弹道导弹潜艇的权宜之计，已经建造了几艘能够携带飞机大小的巡航导弹的潜艇：改装的舰队突尼斯号和巴贝罗号舰船在船尾甲板上安装了外部机库进行试验，每艘机载两艘Regulus-I导弹。随后，两艘“灰背”级柴电巡航导弹潜艇投入使用，每艘都配备了四枚导弹，“比目鱼”级的一艘潜艇则配备了五枚导弹。如果这些船能够携带如此大的导弹，那么美国海军就认为他们可以替代地携带有人驾驶飞机。
   最初的想法是建造可以从现有船上发射的飞机，但不久之后，规模更大的专用AN-1概念诞生了。基于大比目鱼级，AN-1将搭载八架战斗机。该飞机将采用尾翼式垂直起降（VTOL）设计，其总体尺寸与Regulus-II导弹相同。飞机将被装在两个内部吊架中，与“大比目鱼”号上的相似，然后在船浮出水面时从吊架滑出至垂直发射位置。发射助推器将放在单独的较小的吊架中，并与飞机在发射导轨上配合。四架飞机可能会在6分钟内发射完毕，或者八架飞机都将在8分钟内发射完毕。
  获取《世界潜艇》的基本指南《隐秘海岸识别指南》涵盖了80多种潜艇，包括目前在世界海军服役的所有类型。在亚马逊上查看
  AN-1规格排水量：9,260吨浮出水面，14,700吨被淹没长度：498.5 ft横梁：44.25 ft操作深度：tbc速度：16 kt耐力：无限推进力：15,000 shp加压水反应堆机组：149 + 12名飞行员和2架战斗机飞机：8架波音Mach-3 VTOL战斗机装备：6架弓21＆＃34; （533毫米）重量级鱼雷管加上8枚装填弹药和两个船尾短21＆＃34; （533mm）鱼雷周二有六次装弹
 AN-1比大比目鱼大得多，四层甲板全封闭在双层船体中。前机库还装有弓形鱼雷管。单击以获得高分辨率：
  较早的携带潜艇的飞机尽管很少有实例，但携带潜艇的飞机的概念​​并不是新概念。从潜艇发射的第一架飞机是皇家海军HMS M-2的Parnall Peto水上飞机，该飞机于1925年安装了试验性机库。随后，法国海军的Surcouf巡洋舰潜艇于1934年安装，后者的机库是Besson MB .411水上飞机进行侦察。日本人还为一些巡洋舰潜艇配备了水上飞机，最令人印象深刻的设计是I-400，它搭载了三架爱知M6A1 Seiran水上飞机。这些是用于打击任务以及侦察。
  HMS M-2发射了Parnall Peto水上飞机，法国潜艇Surcouf，船尾壳体上装有Besson MB.411水上飞机（还要注意向前的大炮塔），以及日本I-400级水上飞机。
note_en:
In the late 1950s the US Navy experimented with the concept of aircraft carrying submarines armed with jet fighters. Several submarines capable of carrying aircraft-sized cruise missiles had been built as a stop-gap and contingency to ballistic missile submarines: converted fleet boats USS Tunny and Barbero were fitted with external hangars on the aft deck for trials, each carrying two Regulus-I missiles. These were followed by two diesel-electric cruise missile submarines of the  Grayback Class were put into service, each with four missiles, and one submarine of the  Halibut Class with five missiles. If these boats could carry such large missiles, then the US Navy supposed that they could alternatively carry manned aircraft.
   Initially the idea was to built aircraft which could be launched from the existing boats, but soon the larger purpose-designed  AN-1 concept was born. Based on the Halibut Class, the AN-1 would carry eight fighters. The aircraft would be a tail-sitting Vertical Take-Off and Landing (VTOL) design with the same overall size footprint as the Regulus-II missile. The aircraft would be carried in two internal hangers similar to those on USS Halibut, and rolled out of the hanger to vertical launch positions while the boat is surfaced. The launch boosters would be carried in separate smaller hangers and mated with the aircraft on the launch rail. Four aircraft could be launched in under 6 minutes, or all eight in under 8 minutes.

  Earlier aircraft carrying submarines The concept of aircraft carrying submarines was not new although examples are rare. The first aircraft launched from a submarine was a Parnall Peto floatplane from the Royal Navy’s HMS M-2 which was fitted with an experimental hangar in 1925. This was followed by the French Navy’s Surcouf cruiser submarine in 1934 which had a hangar for a Besson MB.411 floatplane for reconnaissance. The Japanese also equipped some cruiser submarines with float planes and the most impressive design was the I-400 which carried three Aichi M6A1 Seiran floatplanes. 这家年轻的公司最近在Product Hunt上进行了试点，今天公开了220万美元的种子资金，此前该公司曾由其两名亚美尼亚创始人，首席执行官Mariam Hakobyan和CTO Artur Mkrtchyan大力支持。领导这一轮的是Atlantic Labs，以及Philipp Moehring（Tiny.VC）和来自GitHub，SumUp，Zeitgold，EyeEm和Rows的创始人。
 从2019年开始，Softr建立了一个无代码平台，使任何人都可以根据Airtable中存储的数据来构建网站和Web应用程序。这个想法是让Airtable负责数据库工作，并结合Softr相对灵活但由模板驱动的网站和Web应用程序创建方法。
  Softr的Hakobyan解释说，这家创业公司开箱即用地提供了模板，可用于从简单的营销网站到电子商务商店，工作委员会，市场等等的Web应用程序的所有内容。这些应用程序可以包括用户身份验证，门控内容，付款，投票和评论等功能。
 她解释说：“ Softr的学习曲线为零，几乎没有技术背景的任何人都可以使用它，因为它抽象了所有技术方面，使用户专注于产品的构建和内容，而不是技术。” “ Softr使用Airtable作为数据库，因为它使创建和共享关系数据库变得容易，而无需学习SQL或脚本。在过去的几年中，Airtable变得非常流行，不仅被个人使用，而且被《财富》 500强公司使用。”
  为此，Hakobyan说，Softr的魔力在于它使用“预先构建的构建块”（列表，用户帐户，付款等）的概念和业务逻辑来代表网站创建者处理大部分繁重的工作。她解释说：“在使用块和模板时，已经为用户完成了70％的工作。”
 此外，Softr还可以连接流行的服务，例如Stripe，Paypal，Mailchimp，Zapier，Integromat，Hotjar，Google Analytics，Hubspot，Drift等。
 目前，“数以千计的制造商和初创公司”使用Softr。客户在Softr上构建的应用程序示例包括具有会员资格的语言学习学校，保姆预订市场以及具有封闭式内容和在线课程的社区。 
借助资金，Softr计划将其客户群扩展到中小型企业的非技术部门，以帮助他们建立内部工具，例如员工目录，产品清单，房地产清单等，并实现手动流程的自动化。
note_en:
Recently soft launched on Product Hunt, today the young company is disclosing $2.2 million in seed funding, having previously been bootstrapped by its two Armenian founders, CEO Mariam Hakobyan and CTO Artur Mkrtchyan. Leading the round is Atlantic Labs, along with Philipp Moehring (Tiny.VC) and founders from GitHub, SumUp, Zeitgold, EyeEm and Rows.
 Started in 2019, Softr has built a no-code platform to enable anybody to build websites and web apps based on data housed in Airtable. The idea is to let Airtable do the database grunt work, combined with Softr’s relatively flexible but template-driven approach to website and web app creation.
  Softr’s Hakobyan explains that out of the box the startup offers templates for anything from a simple marketing website to web apps for an e-commerce store, job board, marketplace and more. Those applications can include functionality like user authentication, gated content, payments, upvoting, and commenting etc.
 “Softr has zero learning curve and can literally be used by anyone without a tech background, as it abstracts away all the technical aspects and focuses the user on product building and content, rather than technology,” she explains. “Softr uses Airtable as the database, as it makes it easy creating and sharing relational databases, without having to learn SQL or scripting. Airtable has gotten pretty popular in the last few years and is used not only by individuals but also Fortune 500 companies”.
  To that end, Hakobyan says Softr’s magic is that it uses the concept of “pre-built building blocks” (listings, user accounts, payments etc) and business logic to handle most of the heavy lifting on behalf of the website creator. “When using blocks and templates.. ., 70% of the work is already done for the user,” she explains.
 In addition, Softr connects to popular services like Stripe, Paypal, Mailchimp, Zapier, Integromat, Hotjar, Google Analytics, Hubspot, Drift and others.
 Softr is currently used by “several thousands of makers and startups”. Examples of applications that customers have built on Softr include a language learning school with membership, a baby-sitters booking marketplace, and a community with gated content and online courses.
 Armed with capital, Softr plans to expand its customer base to non-tech functions of SMBs to help them build internal tooling, such as employee directories, product inventories, real estate listings etc., and to automate manual processes.

有了这个，APX于2018年推出，现在能够为每家公司部署高达500,000欧元的A轮融资。这比该基金成立时的最高10万欧元有所增加。迄今为止，该集团已投资了70多家公司，并计划到2022年将这一数字增加到接近200家。
 APX成立时，该基金没有透露保时捷和Axel Springer的总投资。今天，该团队表示，新投资“将APX投资新公司和现有公司的总金额增加一倍以上。” APX还强调说，该基金的总规模现在“至少”为5500万欧元，部分原因是投资者可以随时为异常值分配额外的资金。
  除了新的资金，APX今天还宣布将取消其100天加速器计划，而是选择对其公司作出长期承诺，包括参与未来的回合。
 “我们今年将尝试向50家或更多公司投资，而去年我们只有35家。因此，这是相当大的增长。” APX创始董事总经理（和民间音乐爱好者）亨里克·汉格霍夫（Henric Hungerhoff）告诉我。 “我们认为我们的交易流程系统和我们的整个运营都已经很好地解决了，我们可以在我们的投资组合中拥有高质量的创始人。这是我们的目标-明年可能会增加到70。 […]我们看到投资组合中确实存在很好的协同作用或网络效应，创始人共同帮助其他创始人并互相学习。”
  Hungerhoff告诉我，该团队现在对确定高质量交易流的能力非常有信心。团队正在使用一种数据驱动的方法。尽管它利用自己的网络和创始人的网络，但它还在欧洲领先的大学中设立了一项侦查计划，以识别潜在的创始人。
 正如APX的创始董事总经理以及Axel Springer的即插即用加速器的前首席执行官一样，JörgRheinboldt指出，APX从未要求其创始人提出建议。取而代之的是，团队与他们进行了多次对话，讨论他们想要构建的产品，他们如何提出想法以及随着时间的变化。
 “然后，我们同时做多件事，”莱茵伯格说。 “一个是，我们关注团队动态。创始人如何互动？我们也以友好的方式向他们强调一点，即有人提出非常快速的问题，或者我们将重点放在一个人身上，看看其他人如何解救他们。我们想了解团队动态，然后想了解策略，我们如何才能最好地帮助他们？” 
这里的想法是能够快速投资。此外，尽管有了新的资金投入，该团队不仅能够投资更多的公司，而且还能向各个公司投资更多。
  亨格霍夫说：“我们希望在早期阶段就对每个初创公司进行更深的投资。” “到目前为止，[…]我们的典型方法是大多数投资组合公司采用非稀释的按比例追加战略。这是我们将来要保证的事情。回顾过去，在100％的股权融资中，我们会按比例进行后续交易，但现在，我们制定了一项战略，我们将针对发展最快，发展最快的公司，希望在早期阶段就大量部署现金，这意味着最多50万欧元。”
 团队看到的是，投资组合中的公司将从APX和其他投资者那里筹集一小笔预种子轮，而APX通常持有这家初创公司5％的股份。然后，大多数创始人会继续进行，并在此后不久进行扩展的种子前或种子轮。
 Rheinbolt说：“当我们看到这些公司筹集了非常不错的融资回合并进行了投资时，我们更感到错过了。” “我们能按比例进行投资感到非常高兴。但是我们互相看着对方说：我们知道这一点，我们知道他们会在12周前这样做。我们可以给他们一张支票，也许一轮会在8周内完成，也许[我们的股份]不是5％，而是7％。”
 鉴于新的重点是在整个生命周期中为初创企业提供支持，APX也取消了为期100天的计划也就不足为奇了。但是车队仍然希望自己动手做。但是，随着网络的不断壮大，合作伙伴还希望创始人也能够互相学习。亨格霍夫说：“我们现在看到了由此带来的价值。”例如，我们两个月前投资的一个团队，现在正在考虑天使回合。实际上，他们可以从另一个团队那里获得最好的建议（或只是经验丰富的分享），而不必与可能在30年前做到这一点的约格（Jörg）交谈，这没有冒犯。”
 该团队还花费大量时间思考其社区，该社区现在包括来自20个国家/地区的创始人。 COVID大流行显然已使大多数互动在线互动。在发布COVID之前，APX经常在其办公室内举办活动，这有助于产生一种偶然性，从而经常带来新的想法和联系。展望未来，团队仍然认为面对面开会具有很大的价值，但同时，也许并非每家公司都需要搬到柏林，而是不时地拜访几天。
note_en:
With this, APX, which  launched in 2018, is now able to deploy up to €500,000 in pre-Series A seed funding per company. That’s up from up to €100,000 when the fund launched. So far, the group has invested in more than 70 companies and plans to increase this number to close to 200 by 2022.
 When APX launched, the fund didn’t disclose the total investment from Porsche and Axel Springer. Today, the team said that the new investment “more than doubles APX’s total amount for investing in new and current companies.” APX also stressed that the total volume of the fund is now “at least” €55 million, in part because the investors can always allocate additional funding for outliers.
  In addition to the new funding, APX also today announced that it is doing away with its 100-day accelerator program and instead opting for a long-term commitment to its companies, including participation in future rounds.
 “We will try and invest into 50 or more companies this year — and we were at 35 last year. So this is quite some growth,” APX founding managing director (and  folk music aficionado) Henric Hungerhoff told me. “We think that our deal flow systems and our entire operations are settled in well enough that we can have quality founders in our portfolio. That’s our goal — and that might even increase to 70 the year after. […] We see really nice synergies or network effects within our portfolio, with founders helping other founders and learning from each other.”
  Hungerhoff tells me that the team is quite confident in its ability now to identify quality deal flows. The team is using a data-driven approach. And while it leverages its own network and that of its founders, it has also set up a scout program at leading European universities to identify potential founders, for example.
 As APX founding managing director, and the former CEO of Axel Springer’s Plug and Play accelerator, Jörg Rheinboldt noted, APX never asks its founders to pitch. Instead, the team has multiple conversations with them about the product they want to build, how they came up with the idea — and how it changed over time.
 “And then, we do multiple things simultaneously,” Rheinboldt said. “One is, we look at team dynamics. How do the founders interact? We also stress them a little bit — in a friendly way — where someone asks very fast questions, or we focus a little bit on one person and see how the others rescue them. We want to know about the team dynamics and then we want to understand the strategy, how we can help them best?”
 The idea here is to be able to invest quickly. In addition, though, with the new funding, the team isn’t just able to invest into more companies but also invest more into the individual companies.
  “We want to invest deeper per startup at a very early stage,” Hungerhoff said. “So far, […] our typical approach was a non-dilution, pro-rata follow-on strategy with most of our portfolio companies. And this is something we want to pledge in the future. Looking at the past, 100% of the times in equity rounds, we do the pro-rata follow-on or more, but now, we have developed a strategy that we will, for the fastest-moving of fastest-growing companies, we want to deploy significantly more cash in a very early phase, which means an amount of up to €500,000.”
 What the team saw was that the companies in its portfolio would raise a small pre-seed round from APX and other investors, with APX typically taking a 5% stake in the startup. Most founders would then go on and raise extended pre-seed or seed rounds soon thereafter.
 “We more felt like we missed out when we saw these companies raising really nice financing rounds and we did our investment,” Rheinbolt said. “We felt very good that we can do a pro-rata investment. but we looked at each other and said: we knew this, we knew that they would do this 12 weeks ago. We could have given them a check and maybe the round would have been done in eight weeks and maybe [our stake] wouldn’t be 5% but 7%.”
 Given this new focus on supporting startups throughout their lifecycle, it’s no surprise that APX did away with the 100-day program as well. But the team still expects to be quite hands-on. With a growing network, though, the partners also expect that founders will be able to learn from each other, too. “We now see the value that is coming from this,” Hungerhoff said. For example, a team that we’ve invested in two months ago, they’re now thinking about the angel round. They can actually get the best advice on this — or just experienced sharing — from another team, rather than talking to Jörg who did this maybe 30 years ago — no offense.”
 The team also spends a lot of time thinking about its community, which now includes founders from 20 countries. The COVID pandemic has obviously moved most of the interactions online. Before COVID, APX often hosted events in its offices, which helped create the kind of serendipity that often leads to new ideas and connections. Looking ahead, the team still believes that there is a lot of value in having face-to-face meetings, but at the same time, maybe not every company needs to move to Berlin and instead visit for a few days every now and then.

from wsgiref.simple_server import make_serverfrom pyramid.config import Configuratorfrom pyramid.response import Responsedef hello_world(request): return Response('Hello World!')if __name__ == '__main__': with Configurator() as config: config.add_route('hello', '/') config.add_view(hello_world, route_name='hello') app = config.make_wsgi_app() server = make_server('0.0.0.0', 6543, app) server.serve_forever()
  Pyramid makes it easy to write web applications. You can  start small with this "hello world" minimal request/response web app. This may take you far, especially while learning. As your application grows, Pyramid offers many features that make writing complex software take less effort.
  Pyramid works in all supported versions of Python. Our  installation instructions will help you get Pyramid up and running.
  Pyramid's  quick tutorial will take you step by step through writing a single file application, forms, database integration, and authentication.
  Developers may dive in to Pyramid's  narrative documentation, or browse the extensive  API reference. Pyramid has a rich pool of helpful  resources from which to draw.  Extending Pyramid is a curated and filterable list of add-ons, packages, and applications built to work with Pyramid.
  Megaframeworks make decisions for you. But if you don't fit their viewpoint, you end up fighting their decisions.  Microframeworks force no decisions, making it easy to start. But as your application grows, you're on your own.
  In both cases, the focus is on the  start: either too much or too little. Either way, finishing and staying finished is hard. You need a  finishing-focused framework with an architectural design that scales down to getting started, then up as your application grows.
  Pyramid was made for just this. It's a Goldilocks Solution: not too small, not too big, just right.
  Getting started quickly and simply is a key attraction of lightweight frameworks. Equally, you get to choose what approaches to use for templating, database, security, and more, or use a convenient starting point with a scaffold. Pyramid excels at scaling down to the first hour of learning, while avoiding the pitfalls of framework magic.
  Choose from a variety of  templating, database, security solutions and more using the quality and convenience of Pyramid's add-on system
  Tap into a variety of  high-quality documentation for evaluating, trying out, or doing advanced development with Pyramid
  Ambitious projects aspire to grow big without losing their dignity. Pyramid is uniquely equipped to scale with you. Its configuration, extension, and add-on system gives the skeleton to support your ambitions, and its architecture ensures that you don't lose those performance and quality features you started with.
  Pyramid's simple first hour helps you get started and its extensability helps you finish your ambitions. There's life after shipping. Pyramid helps keep your application finished by understanding the full life cycle of a professional web application.
  Strong following of  Python practices (WSGI, packages, virtual environments, first to support Python 3) means you won't be out of the Python mainstream
  Full-stack frameworks provide built-in value by telling you what to do. But doing something different, or using something better, leads to the dreaded "fighting the framework". Pyramid starts from a very small base, providing many high-quality choices.
  No bundled templating system but instead, three very  high-quality add-ons for  Chameleon,  Jinja2 and  Mako
  The Pyramid team has been doing ambitious Python web frameworks since 1995. We have built small systems and huge systems. From this, we delight in helping others who appreciate quality and dream big.
  To demonstrate these features,  install Pyramid, click   to expand and copy the code sample into a file, run the application with  env/bin/python demo.py, and use  curl or a browser to request  http://0.0.0.0:6543.
  from wsgiref.simple_server import make_serverfrom pyramid.config import Configuratorfrom pyramid.response import Responsefrom pyramid.view import view_config @view_config( route_name='home')def home(request): return Response('Welcome!') if __name__ == '__main__': with Configurator() as config: config.add_route('home', '/') config.scan() app = config.make_wsgi_app() server = make_server('0.0.0.0', 6543, app) server.serve_forever()
  Pyramid is written in Python. All the features you know and love in the Python language, such as function decorators, are available to Pyramid developers. Here we show the function named  home that returns a response. The function has a decorator  @view_config which has a route assigned to it also named  home.
  from wsgiref.simple_server import make_serverfrom pyramid.config import Configuratorfrom pyramid.response import Responsefrom pyramid.view import view_config @view_config( route_name='home', request_method='POST')def home(request): return Response('Welcome!') if __name__ == '__main__': with Configurator() as config: config.add_route('home', '/') config.scan() app = config.make_wsgi_app() server = make_server('0.0.0.0', 6543, app) server.serve_forever()
  A test which returns  True or  False, and which narrows the set of circumstances in which views or routes may be called. Here we use predicates to limit matching of a view callable to a route name of  home and to the  POST HTTP request method.
  from wsgiref.simple_server import make_serverfrom pyramid.config import Configuratorfrom pyramid.view import view_config @view_config( route_name='home', renderer='json')def home(request): return {"a": 1, "b": 2} if __name__ == '__main__': with Configurator() as config: config.add_route('home', '/') config.scan() app = config.make_wsgi_app() server = make_server('0.0.0.0', 6543, app) server.serve_forever()
  Convert non-response return values that are later consumed by renderers. Using a renderer can make writing views that require templating or other serialization, like  JSON, less tedious.

Copy this template and put it in `templates/home.jinja2`:<!DOCTYPE html><html lang="en"><head> <title>{{ greet }}, {{ name }}</title></head><body><h1>{{ greet }}, {{ name }}</h1></body></html>"""from wsgiref.simple_server import make_serverfrom pyramid.config import Configuratorfrom pyramid.view import view_config @view_config( route_name='home', renderer='templates/home.jinja2')def home(request): return {"greet": 'Welcome', "name": 'Akhenaten'} if __name__ == '__main__': with Configurator() as config: config.include('pyramid_jinja2') config.add_route('home', '/') config.scan() app = config.make_wsgi_app() server = make_server('0.0.0.0', 6543, app) server.serve_forever()
  Allows specifying the location of assets in a package. Here the asset is specified as a Jinja2 template  home.jinja2, located in a subdirectory named  templates. Within a package  myapp, a colon delimits the package name from the location of the asset relative to the package, for example  renderer='myapp:templates/home.jinja2'.
  from wsgiref.simple_server import make_serverfrom pyramid.config import Configuratorfrom pyramid.events import NewRequestfrom pyramid.events import NewResponsefrom pyramid.events import subscriberfrom pyramid.response import Responsefrom pyramid.view import view_config@view_config( route_name='home',)def home(request): return Response('Welcome!') @subscriber(NewRequest, NewResponse)def mysubscriber(event): print(event) if __name__ == '__main__': with Configurator() as config: config.add_route('home', '/') config.scan() app = config.make_wsgi_app() server = make_server('0.0.0.0', 6543, app) server.serve_forever()
  An event is an object broadcast at interesting points during the lifetime of an application. A subscriber to an event allows you to run some code, such as resizing an uploaded image, sending email, or sending a message to a remote system. Here the decorated subscriber will be called for more than one event type, specifically for every new request and response objects.

Cornet将专注于软件和金融科技领域，此前曾领导该公司进行了多项投资，并在五年内从合伙人晋升为合伙人。当VC因拥有“不继承”文化而臭名昭著时，除非合作伙伴有效地购买了资金，否则这是不寻常的职业发展轨迹。
 Cornet评论道：“我对欧洲技术企业家的发展前景非常乐观。我们看到整个欧洲地区越来越多的杰出创始人和创新业务充满抱负和成为全球冠军的能力，我期待着帮助他们扩大规模。”
  Cornet在接受TechCrunch采访时补充说：“我感到非常幸运，因为我认为我们在整个科技行业以及整个欧洲都经历了一生一次的转型。建立其中的一些公司并成为生态系统的一部分真是太棒了。我知道未来几年还会有更多激动人心的事情。”
 Cornet先前曾领导对总部位于巴黎的支出管理平台Spendesk的投资； Thinksurance，总部位于法兰克福的B2B insurtech；以及Compte-Nickel，这是欧洲首批新兴银行之一，于2017年被法国巴黎银行成功收购。她还是VIU Eyewear，OTA Insight和Fuse Universal的董事会成员。
 出生于法国的Cornet的先前职业包括投资银行，Summit Partners，她于2015年加入了八路创投。她是2020年GP Bullhound年度投资者奖的“后起之秀”。
 欧洲八路风险投资公司的执行合伙人达沃·赫贝尔（Davor Hebel）评论说：“我们对Lucile迄今为止在八路的成功感到高兴。自加入公司以来，她在欧洲乃至全球都产生了巨大影响。她具有极大的职业道德和动力……选择最佳的欧洲公司，并帮助他们扩大为全球赢家。她的晋升也表明我们渴望继续发展我们最好的投资人才并从内部晋升。”
 Hebel在接受采访时对我说：“我们一直相信一种略有不同的方法，我们说，当我们雇用员工时，甚至从一开始就希望他们有判断力，并且希望他们在遇到企业家时有这种存在感。因此，这一直是我们所说的模式的一部分，我们可能不会雇用很多人，但我们确实希望他们有潜力与我们一起成长和发展，并拥有这样做的道路和潜力。” 
2020年，欧洲八大风险投资公司对卡索，奥特里姆，Spendesk，Odaseva和最近的蒂伯进行了投资，完成了八项后续投资并退出了里米利亚。 该公司还看到其投资组合公司AppsFlyer的估值达到20亿美元。
note_en:
Cornet will be focusing on the software and fintech sectors and previously led a number of investments for the firm, having risen from Associate to Partner within five years. It’s an out of the ordinary career trajectory when VC is notorious for having a ‘no succession’ culture, unless partners effectively buy into funds.
 Cornet commented: “I am hugely optimistic about what is to come for European technology entrepreneurs. We are seeing more and more amazing founders and innovative businesses across the whole European region with ambitions and abilities to become global champions, and I look forward to helping them scale up.”
  Speaking with TechCrunch, Cornet added: “I feel so, so fortunate because I think we’ve been living during a once in a lifetime transformation in general in tech and also in Europe. To build some of those companies, and just be part of the ecosystem has been fantastic. I know how much more exciting things are going to be in the next couple of years.”
 Cornet previously led investments into Spendesk, the Paris-based spend management platform; Thinksurance, the Frankfurt-based B2B insurtech; and Compte-Nickel, one of the first European neobanks which was successfully acquired by BNP Paribas in 2017. She also sits on the boards of VIU Eyewear, OTA Insight and Fuse Universal.
 France-born Cornet’s previous career includes investment banking, Summit Partners, and she joined Eight Roads Ventures in 2015. She was a ‘rising star’ at the GP Bullhound Investor of the Year Awards 2020.
 Commenting, Davor Hebel, managing partner at Eight Roads Ventures Europe, said: “We are delighted with Lucile’s success so far at Eight Roads. She has made a huge impact in Europe and globally since joining the firm. She has a tremendous work ethic and drive… identifying the best European companies and helping them scale into global winners. Her promotion also speaks to our desire to continue to develop our best investment talent and promote from within.”
 Speaking to me in an interview Hebel added: “We always believed in a slightly different approach and we say when we hire people, even from the start, we want them to have judgment, and we want them to have that presence when they meet entrepreneurs. So it was always part of the model for us to say, we might not hire many people, but we really want them to have the potential to grow and stay with us and have the path and the potential to do so.”
 In 2020, Eight Roads Ventures Europe invested in Cazoo, Otrium, Spendesk, Odaseva and most recently Tibber, completed eight follow-on investments and exited Rimilia. The firm also saw its portfolio company AppsFlyer reach a $2 billion valuation.

title_en: Eight Roads Ventures Europe shifts its gears towards diversity, appointing Lucile Cornet to Partner

加里·努曼（Gary Numan）告诉天空新闻（Sky News），由于对数字音乐服务经济性的调查仍在进行中，因此流传超过一百万次的热门歌曲仅获得37英镑。
 英国歌手兼作词人，最著名的歌曲包括Are＆＃Friends＆＃39; Electric？，Cars and We Are Glass在1970年代和80年代，多年来已售出了数百万张唱片，并将于5月发行他的第18张个人录音室专辑Intruder。
  由于COVID-19大流行消除了巡回演出带来的艺术家收入，过去12个月中，诸如Spotify，Apple Music，Amazon Music和Google Play之类的平台受到了越来越多的审查，目前正由文化，媒体，和体育委员会（DCMS）的查询。
  周三，来自英国三巨头的高管们主要唱片公司-索尼音乐公司，华纳音乐公司和环球音乐公司-以及许可机构的代表，出现在第三届听证会的国会议员面前，听证会上已经看到了诸如肘部前奏者Guy Garvey，Radiohead吉他手Ed O＆＃39之类的艺术家。 Brien，时尚的Nile Rodgers和歌手兼作曲家Nadine Shah提供了证据。
 唱片公司负责人告诉国会议员，他们从流媒体节目中削减的收入是对发展艺术家，录制，营销和发行所涉及的风险的一种公平奖励。
   努曼（Numan）并未出现在调查中，而是在接受《天空新闻》（Sky News）采访时对流媒体业务产生的收益进行了思考-将这些数字放在了背景之中。
  解决方案很简单，他说。 ＆＃34;流媒体公司应该支付更多的钱。他们一无所获。 
＆＃34;前段时间我发表声明，我的一首歌有超过一百万的播放次数，一百万的流，当时是37英镑。我从一百万个视频流中获得了37英镑。
 再举一个例子，努曼继续说：＆＃34;我打印了出来，我想大约是一年前，我发表了一份声明-我的流媒体声明出现了，我没有看，只是把它打印了，半个小时后，我看了看，它仍在打印。
 ＆＃34;这是数百页。最后的价格是112英镑。它几乎不值得在上面打印[纸]，并且花了将近半小时才打印出来。您知道的是，它的东西太多了，流媒体太多了，里面绝对没有任何东西。
   流媒体目前占全球音乐行业收入的一半以上，并为英国带来了超过10亿英镑的收入，去年有1140亿个音乐流。
 但根据《残破唱片》活动的统计，艺术家从流媒体中获得的收入约占总收入的16％，而唱片公司获得的收入约为41％，流媒体服务的收入约为29％。
 努曼（Numan）表示，非常大的艺术家可以通过流媒体做得很好，但是较小的艺术家则在努力。
 ＆＃34;如果您确实处于最高位置，那么您可以从流媒体中获得不错的收入，＆＃34;他说。 ＆＃34;如果不是，您可能会忘记它，甚至不值得将其打印出来，也无需打印语句。 
音乐版权集体唱片公司Phonographic Performance Ltd的首席执行官Peter Leathem在最近的DCMS会议上回荡了明星的话，他说：＆＃34;如果看一下2019，最畅销的专辑是Bohemian的Queen根据电影改编的狂想曲，以及甲壳虫乐队成立50周年的修道院大道。
 ＆＃34;如果您想打破一位新艺人或想让自己的流媒体播放下去，那么您已经拥有了音乐行业的最后50年才能与之抗衡。
     ＆＃34;这是一个不断发展的情况，＆＃34;他说。它被高效而灵活的市场管理着，它不需要任何改变。
 ＆＃34;在我觉得英国需要成为唱片音乐之乡的时候，任何干扰都可能削弱英国的竞争力，就像通过提供第二大出口业务而在全球提供十分之一的音乐流一样。 ＃34;
 在会议期间，有人建议主要标签的运作方式像＆＃34; oligopoly＆＃34; -索尼音乐英国和爱尔兰董事长兼首席执行官Jason Iley回答：＆＃34;独立领域是一个杰出的领域，并标志着一些最好的举动。
 ＆＃34;艺术家有更多机会签署主要标签或签署独立标签或分发自己的唱片。今天有更多的途径比我从事这项工作时见过的要多。
title: 加里·努曼（Gary Numan）：“我的一首歌获得了超过一百万的流-我获得了£37”

本文的目的不是像在本网站上的文章那样专注于一个特定的Web浏览器，而是对每个Web浏览器进行真空评估，而是将彼此对此网站进行了评级的所有Web浏览器进行比较。该排名基于浏览器默认提供多少隐私以及通过配置它可以获得多少隐私的排名。
    所有这些浏览器都需要最少的配置，并且可以使用全面的内容阻止扩展套件实现浏览现代Web兼容性所需的所需隐私级别，这些内容阻止扩展程序可以正确，完全阻止间谍软件提供者。铱和苍白的月亮都以泄漏用户信息的方式进行配置，因此需要其他配置。
      这些浏览器没有隐私问题，但是它们也没有足够的隐私功能以使其达到最高等级。这些浏览器都具有简单的广告拦截器，并且没有任何隐私问题，但是，这些工具不如Top Tier浏览器提供的全面的隐私工具那么好。
      这些浏览器没有任何重大的隐私漏洞，但也没有足够的隐私保护。 Qutebrowser中有一个非常基本的adblocker。两种浏览器均无权访问扩展程序。因此，尽管开发人员未在其浏览器中放置间谍软件，但这还不足以能够私下浏览现代网络。
      这些浏览器无法保护您的隐私，但是由于它们仍具有某些功能，因此它们并不是最低级别的浏览器，尽管通常不应使用它们。 Vivaldi不允许您禁用所有间谍软件功能，勇敢的将白名单跟踪器并强制更新，并且Firefox和Waterfox都装有间谍软件，以至于对其进行配置是如此简单，以至于您最好使用Firefox版本默认情况下，这种做法会尊重您的隐私，而不是潜入挖掘所有间谍软件功能的不确定性（每次更新浏览器时都重复此过程）。在此列表中选择更高的浏览器总是会更好。
      这些浏览器的设计毫不羞耻地收集了尽可能多的有关用户的信息（该站点对它们的评价都很高）。只有SRWare Iron可以使用它的源代码，而且所有开发人员长期以来一直在虐待他们的用户（完全无视隐私和/或虚假广告）。这些浏览器对他们的用户充满敌意，因此完全不应使用。
note_en:
This purpose of this article is not to rate each web browser in a					vacuum, like articles on this website that focus on one specific web					browser, but rather to compare all of the web browsers that have been					rated on this website against each other. This is a ranking that is					based on how much Privacy a browser offers by default, as well as, how					much privacy can be gained by configuring it.
    These are all browsers that require a minimal amount of configuration					and can achieve the required level of privacy needed to browse the					modern web- compatibility with a comprehensive suite of					content-blocking extensions that can block spyware providers correctly					and fully. Iridium and Pale Moon both are configured in a way that					leaks user information and thus require additional configuration.
      These browsers do not have privacy issues, but they also do not have					enough privacy features to make it to the highest tier. These browsers					both have simple ad-blockers, and do not have any privacy issues,					however, these tools are not as good as the comprehensive privacy					tools that Top Tier browsers offer.
      These browsers do not have any big privacy flaws, but they also do not					have sufficient privacy protections. Qutebrowser has a very basic					adblocker in it. Both browsers don't have access to extensions either.					So, it's just not enough to be able to browse the modern web					privately, despite the developers not putting spyware into their					browsers.
      These browsers do not protect your privacy, but they are not in the					lowest tier since they still have something to offer, although, they					should not be used in general. Vivaldi does not let you disable all of					the spyware features, Brave whitelists trackers and has forced					updates, and Firefox and Waterfox are loaded with spyware, to the					point where configuring them is so					 non-trivial that you					might as well use a version of Firefox that respects your privacy					 by default, rather than diving into the uncertainty of digging					out all of the spyware features (and repeating the process every time					the browser is updated). It's always going to be better to pick a					browser higher on this list.
      These browsers are unashamedly designed to collect as much information					about the user as possible (all are rated EXTREMELY HIGH by the site).					Only SRWare Iron has it's source code available, and all the					developers have mistreated their users (complete disregard for privacy					and / or false advertising) for a long time. These browsers are					actively hostile against their users and thus should not be used at					all.
